metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the false premise in AC4: either confirm `docs/your-first-change.md` is created by a `depends_on` sibling story (and name it), or remove the tutorial link-retarget from this story's scope and AC.
findings.finding: `docs/your-first-change.md` does not exist in the repository, but the plan's Scope section asserts it as a live file ('retarget `docs/your-first-change.md`'s vote-semantics link') and AC4 asserts it must link `review-policy.md`. If this file does not exist, the AC is unverifiable and the deliverable is silently dropped or must be created from scratch — neither of which is acknowledged.
findings.location: Scope section ('tutorial link retarget'); AC4 ('`docs/your-first-change.md` links review-policy.md')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the `docs/your-first-change.md` reference: either (a) add a `depends_on` the story that creates it and gate this story's AC4 on that prerequisite, or (b) remove/replace the link-retarget edit target with an existing file (e.g. `CONTRIBUTING.md` §2c already carries the vote-semantics table this story is slimming).
findings.finding: `docs/your-first-change.md` does not exist in the repository, but the plan names it as a live edit target in two places — the Links section ('retarget `docs/your-first-change.md`'s vote-semantics link') and AC4 ('`docs/your-first-change.md` links review-policy.md'). Attempting to retarget a link in a non-existent file will either fail silently or force the implementer to create the file, which is unscoped work.
findings.location: Links section ('retarget `docs/your-first-change.md`'s vote-semantics link from §2c to the new doc') and AC line 4 ('`docs/your-first-change.md` links review-policy.md')
findings.suggested_fix: Since the tutorial file does not exist and is the product of a different story, scope AC4 as 'if `docs/your-first-change.md` exists, it links review-policy.md; otherwise this AC is deferred' — or add an explicit `depends_on` the tutorial story and note it as a prerequisite.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `coverage-gap (merge-review)` row to the vote-semantics table spec, grounded in `voter.py`'s `_merge_coverage_gap_decision()` (not `_TAG_SUFFIXES`), explaining it as a merge-change-specific infra veto when the merge-path REST fetch fails.
findings.finding: The plan's mechanism for the vote-semantics table is sourced exclusively from `_TAG_SUFFIXES` in `adapter.py`, but a sixth distinct tag `[LLM-Review: BLOCK — coverage-gap (merge-review)]` is emitted live in production by `voter.py`'s `_merge_coverage_gap_decision()` function (line 160), which bypasses `_TAG_SUFFIXES` entirely. A contributor who receives this tag — specifically the most complex contributor action (a merge-back change hitting a REST failure) — finds it undocumented in the policy the plan creates, defeating the policy's core purpose for that case.
findings.location: What section — vote-semantics table spec: 'sourced from the authoritative enum _TAG_SUFFIXES in src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py'
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section's table spec, after listing the five _TAG_SUFFIXES coverage-gap sub-reasons, add a sixth row: `coverage-gap (merge-review)` — sourced from `voter.py`'s `_merge_coverage_gap_decision()`, meaning the merge-change review's REST fan-out failed; same response as other coverage-gap variants (infra, not your code; maintainer re-triggers).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the `docs/your-first-change.md` edit target: either (a) add it as an explicit dependency (`depends_on` the story that creates it) with a note that this story's link-retarget is conditional on that story landing first, or (b) remove it from Scope IN and AC4 if no such story exists, keeping only the CONTRIBUTING.md pointer as the outbound link.
findings.finding: The plan's mechanism for AC4 ('docs/your-first-change.md links review-policy.md') and the body's 'retarget docs/your-first-change.md's vote-semantics link from §2c to the new doc' targets a file that does not exist in the repository. The precondition for this edit step is false: there is no `docs/your-first-change.md` to retarget. An implementer following the plan as written will either silently skip this step (AC4 not met), create an out-of-scope file (scope creep), or be blocked on a missing-file ambiguity.
findings.location: Links section ('retarget docs/your-first-change.md's vote-semantics link'); Scope IN ('docs/your-first-change.md link retarget'); AC4 ('docs/your-first-change.md links review-policy.md')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `coverage-gap (merge-review)` to the vote-semantics table in `docs/review-policy.md` — this tag is emitted by `voter.py`'s `_merge_coverage_gap_decision` (hardcoded, not in `_TAG_SUFFIXES`) and must be sourced from `voter.py` in addition to `adapter.py`.
findings.finding: The plan instructs the doc to enumerate all coverage-gap tag variants by sourcing `_TAG_SUFFIXES` in `adapter.py`, but a sixth coverage-gap tag — `[LLM-Review: BLOCK — coverage-gap (merge-review)]` — is emitted by `voter.py` via a hardcoded string in `_merge_coverage_gap_decision` (line 160) and does NOT appear in `_TAG_SUFFIXES`. The plan conflates this with the `(merge-change, N integrated commits)` suffix variant, which is a different feature. A doc built strictly from `_TAG_SUFFIXES` will be incomplete, causing contributors who see a `merge-review` coverage-gap to not find it in the policy table.
findings.location: What section — vote-semantics table; `src/rebar/review_bot/voter.py` line 160; `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py` lines 44–52
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, amend the tag-source sentence to: 'sourced from `_TAG_SUFFIXES` in `adapter.py` (five coverage-gap sub-reasons: gate-disabled, llm-unavailable, scanner, review-error, indeterminate) AND the hardcoded `merge-review` tag in `voter.py`'s `_merge_coverage_gap_decision`.' Add a sixth row for `coverage-gap (merge-review)` to the semantics table.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `coverage-gap (merge-review)` as an explicit sixth row in the vote-semantics table (sourced from `voter.py:_merge_coverage_gap_decision()`, not `_TAG_SUFFIXES`), and add 'merge-review' to the AC grep list alongside 'merge-change'.
findings.finding: `coverage-gap (merge-review)` is a production-emitted sixth coverage-gap tag variant — assembled in `voter.py:_merge_coverage_gap_decision()` outside `_TAG_SUFFIXES` — that the plan's vote-semantics table omits entirely. The plan claims to document 'all FIVE coverage-gap sub-reasons' sourced from `_TAG_SUFFIXES`, but this merge-path infra-failure tag is emitted by a separate code path and will not appear in the table, leaving contributors unable to recognize it.
findings.location: What section — vote-semantics table / AC grep list / `src/rebar/review_bot/voter.py:160` vs `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py:44-52`
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, change 'all FIVE coverage-gap sub-reasons (gate-disabled, llm-unavailable, scanner, review-error, indeterminate)' to 'six coverage-gap tag values: five from `_TAG_SUFFIXES` (gate-disabled, llm-unavailable, scanner, review-error, indeterminate) plus `merge-review` emitted by `voter.py:_merge_coverage_gap_decision()` for merge-path infra failures'. Add 'merge-review' to the AC grep list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand AC1 to list the exact anchored grep patterns for each required term (e.g., `grep -E 'coverage-gap \(gate-disabled\)'`) and enumerate the five sub-reason strings inline in the AC so the proving command is self-contained.
findings.finding: AC1's proving command is underspecified for false-positive risk: 'prove: greps for both label names, all five coverage-gap sub-reasons + "merge-change", "recheck", "5 business days", "plan-review"' does not provide anchored grep patterns, so a bare `grep recheck` or `grep scanner` would match instructional prose that mentions a term in passing (e.g., 'do not recheck until CI finishes') without the term being documented as a vote-semantics entry.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'prove: greps for … all five coverage-gap sub-reasons' with: 'prove: the following greps all exit 0 on docs/review-policy.md — `grep -F 'coverage-gap (gate-disabled)'`, `grep -F 'coverage-gap (llm-unavailable)'`, `grep -F 'coverage-gap (scanner)'`, `grep -F 'coverage-gap (review-error)'`, `grep -F 'coverage-gap (indeterminate)'`, `grep -F 'merge-change'`, `grep -F 'recheck'`, `grep -F '5 business days'`, `grep -F 'plan-review'`.  For structural sections, grep for the section heading string.'
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the escalation channel in the plan (e.g., 'open a Gerrit comment addressed to the maintainer' or 'file a GitHub issue tagged `policy`') so the executor writes normative text that matches the maintainer's intent.
findings.finding: The dispute-escalation mechanism is unspecified: the plan says contributors should 'raise to the maintainer' but does not state the channel (Gerrit comment, GitHub issue, email). An executor writing the policy doc must invent this detail, which becomes normative for contributors.
findings.location: What section — 'dispute path — resolve the comment thread with written justification or raise to the maintainer'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the dispute-path bullet: 'Escalation channel: reply to the Gerrit change's comment thread addressing the maintainer by name (e.g., @<maintainer>); if the change is abandoned, open a GitHub issue tagged `policy`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a grep-verifiable string (e.g. 'treat it as coverage-gap' or 'ping the maintainer') to AC1's proof list so the drift guard note is covered by an acceptance criterion.
findings.finding: The 'What' section describes a 'drift guard note' deliverable ('if you see a tag not listed here, treat as coverage-gap-class and ping the maintainer') to be included in docs/review-policy.md, but no AC covers this deliverable — AC1's grep list does not include a verifiable string for it.
findings.location: What section (drift guard note paragraph) vs. Acceptance Criteria AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC1: '…and the drift-guard note (grep for "treat it as coverage-gap" or equivalent).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/README.md` to the plan's edit targets (IN scope) with an instruction to add a `review-policy.md` entry under the Contributor section.
findings.finding: `docs/README.md` (the audience-indexed docs table of contents, linked from the top-level `README.md`) is an unenumerated consumer: it lists contributor-facing docs individually under its Contributor section. Adding `docs/review-policy.md` as a new file without adding an entry there leaves the index stale and the doc undiscoverable for contributors browsing the index.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope IN list: '`docs/README.md` — add `review-policy.md` entry under the Contributor section.' Add a corresponding AC grep: 'docs/README.md references review-policy.md'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm `docs/plan-review-gate.md` already exists (or add it as an explicit `depends_on`), and add an AC grep for the linked path to prevent ship-with-missing-target.
findings.finding: The new doc links `docs/plan-review-gate.md` in its distinguishing paragraph, but the plan does not confirm that file exists at ship time, nor does it list plan-review-gate.md as a dependency. If the file is absent, the link is dangling.
findings.location: What section — 'One clarifying paragraph distinguishing THIS (code-review merge gate) from the plan-review ticket gate, linking docs/plan-review-gate.md.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: '`docs/plan-review-gate.md` exists at merge time — prove: `ls docs/plan-review-gate.md` in CI or reference the story that creates it as a hard dependency.' Alternatively, replace the bare link with a conditional: 'see `docs/plan-review-gate.md` (forthcoming)' if the story is genuinely independent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen the 'first-of-kind' prior-art claim by either documenting a brief reproducible search methodology (e.g., GitHub code search + Google Scholar terms checked) or softening it to 'no widely-documented precedent found' with a note that the conclusion (document it) holds regardless.
findings.finding: The plan's central framing motivation — 'research (2024–2026) found no project documenting a blocking LLM merge vote' — is asserted as an empirical fact but cites only one counter-example (Kubernetes, advisory-only). No search methodology or additional sources are provided. If blocking-LLM-review policies exist elsewhere (e.g., in private enterprise OSS, or newer 2025-2026 projects), the 'novelty raises the documentation bar' rationale is weakened, though the conclusion to document it remains sound.
findings.location: Why section — first paragraph: 'rebar's blocking LLM-Review Gerrit label appears to be first-of-kind in public OSS: research (2024–2026) found no project documenting a blocking LLM merge vote'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'appears to be first-of-kind in public OSS: research (2024–2026) found no project documenting a blocking LLM merge vote' to 'no widely-documented precedent was found in surveyed public OSS (Kubernetes, Chromium, Zuul ecosystems) for a *blocking* LLM merge vote — the closest analogue, the Kubernetes AI-review policy, is advisory-only.' This scopes the claim to surveyed sources without asserting exhaustive novelty.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] (Retracted — the plan's What section does enumerate all five sub-reasons including `indeterminate`; this is a non-finding.)
findings.finding: The plan's What section inline enumeration of coverage-gap sub-reasons lists only four values ('gate-disabled, llm-unavailable, scanner, review-error') but the plan's own claim is 'all FIVE' and the AC grep requires 'indeterminate' — the `indeterminate` sub-reason is present in `_TAG_SUFFIXES` and in the AC but silently absent from the What section's enumeration, creating an internal inconsistency that an implementor writing the doc from the What section alone will produce an incomplete table.
findings.location: What section — 'all FIVE coverage-gap sub-reasons (gate-disabled, llm-unavailable, scanner, review-error)' inline list vs AC grep for 'indeterminate'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
